name: Team, Inc.

profile.description: Team, Inc. provides specialty industrial services including mechanical, heat-treating, and inspection services globally through its TEAM360 platform. The company operates in two segments: Inspection & Heat Treating (IHT) and Mechanical Services (MS), serving refining, petrochemical, power generation, aerospace, mining, and pipeline industries across 13 countries.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (0/3): Team's business model has been fundamentally unchanged for decades — the company provides inspection, heat treating, and mechanical services to industrial customers with no meaningful product launches or market pivots in the last five years. The rebranding of service lines under the TEAM360 umbrella is marketing, not innovation [1]. AI Deployment (0/2): No evidence of AI-powered products or quantified AI-driven cost savings. The ONEINSIGHT connected worker platform provides digital field service management but is not AI in a meaningful revenue-generating sense [2]. Market Share (1/2): Team is a meaningful player in North American industrial services with $896M in 2025 revenue and operations in 13 countries, but it is not a category leader or fastest share gainer — the company competes against larger players like Mistras Group and Bureau Veritas [1]. Pipeline (0/2): No specific next-generation products with launch timelines have been announced. The 2026 strategy focuses on 'accelerating commercial execution' in existing service lines, particularly aerospace and power markets [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): Industrial services companies have inherently low R&D intensity; Team's capex of $9.3M in 2025 was directed at maintenance rather than product development [1].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Team positions itself as 'The Leading Global Asset Integrity & Performance 360 Solutions Provider,' which is generic corporate language interchangeable with any industrial services peer — it describes what the company does, not why it exists [2]. Community Impact (1/2): Team publishes annual ESG/sustainability profiles and emphasizes emissions reduction through its Detect360 leak detection services. The company operates a TEAM Technical School for employee development [3]. However, no distinctive community programs, foundation activity, or specific dollar amounts for charitable giving are evident. Employee Retention (0/2): No publicly available data on voluntary turnover, average tenure, or employee satisfaction metrics. The company has approximately 5,300 employees but provides no retention statistics. Insider Buying (1/2): Director Evan Lederman purchased 600 shares in May 2026 at $16.31, and CEO purchases were reported in early 2026 during a modest insider buying period [4]. The amounts are small but signal some leadership confidence in the turnaround.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Infrastructure score is 0 due to the liquidity floor rule: average daily volume of 9,306 shares × $16.68 stock price = $155,182 daily dollar volume, far below the $1,000,000 minimum threshold [5]. Even excluding the liquidity floor, the underlying metrics are deeply distressed. D/E of 2,318.9% reflects $306.5M in total debt against a stockholders' deficit of -$39.4M [1]. ROE is unavailable (negative equity). Profit margin is -3.4% with a full-year 2025 net loss of $49.2M [1]. Forward P/E is -28.8 (negative earnings expected). The company generated negative FCF of -$20.6M in 2025. Revenue growth of 8.3% YoY in Q1 2026 and full-year 2026 guidance of $920-945M (~4% growth at midpoint) show modest improvement, but the balance sheet remains severely overleveraged with the September 2025 preferred stock issuance of $75M adding another layer of capital structure complexity [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Team's core business — on-site industrial inspection, heat treating, leak repair, and mechanical services — is fundamentally physical work that AI cannot replace. Certified inspectors must physically access pipes, pressure vessels, and heat exchangers in refineries and petrochemical plants. The work requires NDE certifications, hands-on expertise, and presence in hazardous environments that no AI or robotic system can currently replicate at scale [2]. Regulatory requirements (ASME, API, NDE codes) mandate human-performed inspections for critical infrastructure. AI may enhance inspection accuracy through computer vision-assisted defect detection, but this augments rather than replaces Team's workforce. The physical, regulated, and safety-critical nature of these services provides near-total immunity to AI disruption. AI Opportunity (0/1): Team does not generate revenue from AI products. The ONEINSIGHT platform is a digital field management tool, not an AI product, and no AI-driven revenue has been reported [2].

infinite_game.summary: Team has a 50+ year heritage dating to the 1920s Furmanite legacy, but the company has spent the last decade in financial distress — navigating post-COVID challenges, massive debt loads, and recurring net losses. The January 2026 CEO transition from Keith Tucker to Gary Hill signals organizational change, but the company's focus on quarterly turnaround metrics and debt management reflects finite-game survival mode rather than infinite-game purpose.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Team's stated purpose — 'asset integrity and performance optimization' — describes a service offering, not a Just Cause. It is functional language about what the company does, not an aspirational vision of a better future that inspires sacrifice. The company's 50+ year history provides continuity, but the strategic narrative centers on financial recovery and 'accelerating commercial execution' rather than advancing a cause [1][2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Team's 5,300 employees operate across 13 countries, and the orderly CEO transition from Keith Tucker (20+ years with the company) to Gary Hill suggests organizational stability [1]. Tucker's 33 years of industry experience and internal promotion history indicate a culture that develops leaders from within. The TEAM Technical School provides structured employee development. However, no employee satisfaction data, voluntary turnover rates, or culture metrics are publicly available, preventing a higher score.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No evidence of an infinite competitive mindset. Team's competitive language focuses on being 'the leading global provider' and market positioning rather than learning from rivals' strengths. The company competes against Mistras Group, Bureau Veritas, and Intertek but does not publicly acknowledge competitors' strengths as motivation for self-improvement [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Team has evolved from its original Furmanite leak-sealing heritage into a broader industrial services platform, integrating inspection, heat treating, and mechanical services under the TEAM360 brand [2]. The recent strategic push into aerospace and power generation markets represents adjacent-market expansion. However, these are incremental evolutions of the same industrial services business model, not transformative pivots. The company has not fundamentally reinvented itself.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Keith Tucker guided Team through a critical post-COVID period, executing a March 2025 debt refinancing that reduced interest rates by over 100 basis points and completing a $75M preferred stock issuance in September 2025 to strengthen the capital structure [1]. The planned leadership transition to Gary Hill in January 2026 was orderly rather than crisis-driven. However, the company's persistent net losses ($49.2M in 2025) and severely overleveraged balance sheet suggest these were survival moves rather than courageous long-term investments.

investment_thesis: Team Inc. offers a turnaround narrative with 8.3% Q1 2026 revenue growth, improving Adjusted EBITDA margins (3.6% in Q1 2026 vs. 2.7% prior year), and full-year 2026 guidance of $920-945M in revenue with $68-73M Adjusted EBITDA representing 16% growth [1]. The business provides essential, physical industrial services — inspection, heat treating, and mechanical repair — that are virtually immune to AI disruption and benefit from aging industrial infrastructure requiring ongoing maintenance. New CEO Gary Hill brings fresh strategic focus on aerospace and power generation markets. The Inspire Insight score of 49 with 14 positive ESG attributions and zero negative attributions reflects strong environmental and social alignment, particularly through the company's emissions detection services [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case is dominated by a catastrophically overleveraged balance sheet: D/E of 2,318.9%, total debt of $306.5M against a stockholders' deficit of -$39.4M, and recurring net losses ($49.2M in 2025, $11.3M in Q1 2026) [1]. The stock is essentially uninvestable for IOWN due to extreme illiquidity — average daily volume of just 9,306 shares produces only ~$155K in daily dollar volume, far below the $1M minimum threshold. Free cash flow remains negative at -$20.6M for 2025 and -$11.5M in Q1 2026 alone. The $75M preferred stock issuance adds another layer of dilution risk. Revenue growth of ~4% at the midpoint of 2026 guidance barely outpaces inflation, and profit margins remain negative. Until the balance sheet is materially de-risked and liquidity improves by an order of magnitude, Team is a speculative turnaround play unsuitable for the IOWN framework.
key_catalysts: Full-year 2026 guidance of $68-73M Adjusted EBITDA representing 16% growth, with gross margin expansion to $240-260M as cost reduction initiatives take hold under new CEO Gary Hill [1]
Strategic expansion into aerospace and power generation markets could diversify revenue beyond traditional refining and petrochemical customers, which face cyclical demand patterns [1]
March 2025 debt refinancing reduced interest expense by over 100 bps, and further de-leveraging could materially improve net income trajectory if EBITDA growth continues [1]
key_risks: Extreme illiquidity with average daily volume of only 9,306 shares ($155K daily dollar volume) makes the stock essentially untradeable for institutional investors and creates severe execution risk on any position [5]
Catastrophic leverage at 2,318.9% D/E with $306.5M debt against negative stockholders' equity of -$39.4M — any revenue shortfall or interest rate increase could trigger a liquidity crisis [1]

Persistent net losses ($49.2M in 2025) with negative free cash flow and a complex capital structure including preferred stock creates ongoing dilution risk and limits the company's ability to invest in growth [1]
